title: Cirrus: Give more memory to golangci-lint
body:
/kind failing-test 

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Increase memory available to `golangci-lint` so that it isn't randomly OOMkilled.

#### How to verify it

Run the automated tests

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

None

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

None

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [cevich] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

```
Failed to start: Invalid value for field 'resource.machineType': 'https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/buildah/zones/us-central1-c/machineTypes/custom-2-16384'. Memory size for 2 vCPU instance should be between 2048MiB and 13312MiB, while 16384MiB is requested
```

One question otherwise 
LGTM
and happy green test buttons.  (Maybe never question success @TomSweeneyRedHat ?)

I mean, it clearly seems to help, but I do wonder if we need to go all the way from 2 vcpus with 4GB of memory to 8 vcpus with 32GB of memory to get that result.  Have we still been seeing the problem with values in between, say 12GB, which is within the bounds of what we can request with a 2 vcpu instance?
If @nalind change works, do we still need this one?
Well, that was a guess, not based on profiling or any data.

Mine is a educated guess as well.  Educated based on hitting something similar in podman, and finding the 16-18gig to be about  the sweet-spot.  The gating tests run so quickly, I'm not overly concerned about "overshooting", and the chance of memory needs growing over time seems greater than it reducing.  That was my thought process.
Rebased, changed to 4CPU/16Gig as maybe a sweet-spot.  Leaving this open for a little while, just in case it's needed (Nalin's 12gig is found to be insufficient).
We can close this at start of Nov. if there are no further OOM killing instances in gate tests...and If I forget to close it sooner :smile:
Since we are past halloween, I am going to close this.  Cirrus seems to be working well.
